close_reason: Converted free fn fetch() to BitwardenBackend::fetch() method
description: src/lib.rs:283 declares 'async fn fetch(backend: &BitwardenBackend)'. Two callers: SecretStore::get and SecretStore::refresh, both inside this crate. The function is private and operates entirely on backend's own fields. Idiomatic Rust would make this an inherent method: 'impl BitwardenBackend { async fn fetch(&self) -> Result<SecretValue, SecretError> { ... } }', then SecretStore::get becomes 'self.fetch().await'. Reasons to prefer the method form: (1) consistent with the rest of the codebase (from_uri is already a method), (2) makes the call site shorter at the use site (self.fetch() vs fetch(self)), (3) signals that fetch is part of BitwardenBackend's behavior, not a free function. The current free-function form is the older C-style API design. Marginal but a real readability/type-design win.
title: fetch() takes &BitwardenBackend; should be a method on BitwardenBackend
